title: Isolation of Phytopathogenic Fungi
author: Daisy Perez-Brito
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.sgsebwe

steps.title: Disinfest by immersing them in 3% NaOCl solution
steps.instruction: Disinfest by immersing them in 3% NaOCl solution. 3% NaOCl solution Contributed by users

steps.title: Transfer each small piece onto 90...
steps.instruction: Transfer each small piece onto 90 x 15 mm Petri Dish containing Potato Dextrose Agar (PDA) medium.

steps.title: Incubate at room temperature (25°C) for 7 days
steps.instruction: Incubate at room temperature (25°C) for 7 days.

steps.title: Cut diseased plant tissues, taken from...
steps.instruction: Cut diseased plant tissues, taken from the advanced margin of lesions, into small pieces (5 × 5 mm) with a scalpel.

steps.title: Cut in the edge of each...
steps.instruction: Cut in the edge of each fungi colony, with a scalpel a mycelial segment of approximately 5 x 5 mm.

steps.title: Transfer each small mycelial piece onto...
steps.instruction: Transfer each small mycelial piece onto 90 x 15 mm Petri dish containing water-agar medium (2%). water-agar medium (2%) Contributed by users

steps.title: Incubate at room temperature (25°C) for 5 days
steps.instruction: Incubate at room temperature (25°C) for 5 days.

steps.title: Observe the fungal colony on a...
steps.instruction: Observe the fungal colony on a stereo microscope and with an insulin syringe needle (29 gauge) cut one hyphae tip.

steps.title: Transfer hyphae tip onto 90 x...
steps.instruction: Transfer hyphae tip onto 90 x 15 mm Petri Dish containing Potato Dextrose Agar (PDA) medium.

steps.title: Incubate at room temperature (25°C) for 7 days
steps.instruction: Incubate at room temperature (25°C) for 7 days.
